name: Cartesian
comment:
A module for cartesian structure

This is an experiment in organizing wiring diagrams

We're not implementing this paper exactly, but finding some inspiration:

The Cost of Compositionality: A High-Performance Implementation of String Diagram Composition

<https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.09257>


## Types

@docs C, Interface


## Usage

@docs before, aside, init, initWith, unit, group, interface, wrap

aliases.name: C
aliases.comment:
The type of a cartesian structure

This is not your typical category theory monoidal thing, as we store a recursive structure

aliases.name: Interface
aliases.comment: The Interface type describes the Arity of the cartesian system structure

values.name: aside
values.comment:
Put two structures aside each other

The structures will be independent

Note the order of arguments are flipped to support this syntax:

    a |> aside b

This will put `a` 'first'
values.type: Cartesian.C a -> Cartesian.C a -> Cartesian.C a

values.name: before
values.comment:
Put one structure before another

The structures will be dependent and must match in the interface

Note the order of arguments are flipped to support this syntax:

    a |> before b

This will put `a` 'first'
values.type: Cartesian.C a -> Cartesian.C a -> Cartesian.C a

values.name: group
values.comment:
Group a structure

This may be used to group substructures

values.name: init
values.comment: Initialize a simple 1->1 arity morphism with label 'a'

values.name: initWith
values.comment: Initialize a morphism with a given interface and label

values.name: interface
values.comment: Extract the outermost interface of the structure

values.name: unit
values.comment:
The unit value

This is possibly not needed, but we'll start off using it.

This may disappear later.

values.name: wrap
values.comment: Wrap a part of the structure with a label a

name: Cartesian.Layout

values.name: toLayout
values.comment:
Layout a cartesian structure

This is unfinished, and won't do anything interesting yet.

values.name: toLayoutWithConfig
values.comment:
Layout with some configuration

This is unfinished, and won't do anything interesting yet.
values.type: Diagram.Layout.Config.Config a -> Internal.Cartesian.C a -> Internal.WiringDiagram.Layout.Layout a

values.name: setLeafExtent

name: Diagram.Vec2

A simple Vec2 type for geometry

values.name: translate

name: WiringDiagram

aliases.name: Diagram
aliases.comment:
Represent an abstract wire-diagram.

Think of boxes connected with ports, that can be inside other boxes (which connect outside)
(TODO illustration)

values.name: diagram
values.comment: Wrap a diagram, exposing the free in and out ports
values.type: { a | label : Maybe.Maybe b, inner : List.List (WiringDiagram.Diagram b) } -> WiringDiagram.Diagram b

values.name: inParallel
values.comment:
Put diagrams in parallel.

No connections will be made.

(We probably need to relabel ports here...)

values.name: inSequence
values.comment: Connect diagrams in a chain

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialize an empty Diagram. The idea is a box with no label.

The box is abstract and will be given geometry using [the Layout module](@Layout)

Use modifiers like `setInPorts` to configure further

values.name: initLabeled
values.comment:
Initialize an empty Diagram with a label of a chosen type

All diagrams you intend to combine needs to have the same label type

values.name: initWrap
values.comment: Wrap a diagram, exposing the free in and out ports

values.name: relation
values.comment: Make a relation with label a and a number of inPorts and outPorts

values.name: setLabel
values.comment:
Assign a label to a Diagram

    setLabel (Just "node") <| diagram --
values.type: Maybe.Maybe a -> WiringDiagram.Diagram a -> WiringDiagram.Diagram a

values.name: sink
values.comment: Make a sink with label a and a number of inPorts

values.name: source
values.comment: Make a source with label a and a number of outPorts

name: WiringDiagram.Examples
comment:
Some example diagrams

@docs sampleDiagrams, a, b, c, a2, b2, c2, twoLanes

values.name: a
values.comment:
A simple source labeled 'a'

It has one output port.

values.name: a2
values.comment:
A simple source labeled 'a2'

It has two output ports

values.name: b
values.comment:
A simple relation labeled 'b'

It has one input and one output port

values.name: b2
values.comment:
A simple relation labeled 'b2'

It has two input and output ports

values.name: c
values.comment:
A simple sink labeled 'c'

It has one input port

values.name: c2
values.comment:
A simple sink labeled 'c2'

It has two input ports

values.name: sampleDiagrams
values.comment:
A list of some diagrams

    sampleDiagrams : List Diagram
    sampleDiagrams =
        [ inSequence [ a, b, c ]
        , inSequence [ wrap a, wrap b ]
        , inSequence [ wrap a, wrap (inParallel [ b, c |> offsetPorts 1 ]) ]
        , wrap_a
        , inSequence
            [ wrap (source "a" 2)
            , sink "b" 2
            ]
        , wrap_axb_c
        , inSequence [ a, b ]
        , inSequence [ source "a3" 3, sink "b2" 2 ]
        ]

values.name: twoLanes
values.comment:
Two sequenced diagrams in parallel

    inParallel
        [ inSequence [ a, c ]
        , inSequence [ source "d" 1, sink "e" 1 ]
        ]

name: WiringDiagram.Layout

aliases.comment: The Layout type keeps information about the layout of a Wiring diagram.

aliases.name: Polarity

values.name: incoming
values.comment: Polarity of a Port that is incoming
